name: Kids Cab
districts_or_customers: Bloomfield, NJ area families; likely after-school / private school transport rather than yellow-bus contract
recent_news: No M&A news found; listed in local directories as a school bus / kids taxi service at 12 Emerson Ter, Bloomfield, NJ.
website: kidcab.biz (no substantive content; directory listings only)
fit_notes: Sub-scale micro operator (~$0.4M revenue, ~9 employees, likely vans/sedans not yellow buses). Not a viable Summit tuck-in; possibly a chaperoned-ride/kid-taxi service rather than a contracted school-bus operator.

name: Lakes Region Student Trnsprtn
owner_or_parent: likely affiliated with Lakes Region Airport Shuttle Service LLC (same phone/address, Northfield NH)
fleet_size_est: small fleet of sedans/vans/shuttles (no yellow buses confirmed)
districts_or_customers: Plymouth State, UNH, Colby-Sawyer; no confirmed K-12 district contracts. Website lrstnh.com redirects to lrairportshuttle.com.
recent_news: No M&A news found. Cortera lists $500K-$1M revenue, 5-10 employees, founded 2003.
fit_notes: Micro shuttle/college-transport operator, not a contracted K-12 yellow-bus contractor. Not a fit as a Summit tuck-in; mis-classified as school-bus in source data.

name: Richmond Transportation
fleet_size_est: small fleet of trolleys, limos, and party buses
districts_or_customers: Greater LA area special-event clientele; no contracted school district business found. A 2003 El Rancho USD (Pico Rivera) board minute references a 'Richmond Bus Company' but no current contract evidence.
recent_news: No M&A news. Operates as Richmond Trolley, Limousine & Transportation Services at 8927 Dalewood Ave, Pico Rivera.
fit_notes: Not a yellow-bus contractor — limo/trolley/charter for special events. Mis-classified as school-bus. Not a Summit tuck-in target.

name: Pollard Bus Co
owner_or_parent: independent — operates as DBA of Estes Transportation (USDOT 820930); founding owners Clarence & Margaret Pollard (Clarence died Dec 2023). Current ownership likely Estes family (Bob Estes listed as owner).
employees_est: ~38 drivers per founder obit (source data employees=1000 is data error)
districts_or_customers: Roswell Independent School District (RISD) — exclusive in-city-limits contractor; partners with Hamill Transportation (outside city limits). Holds NM Charter Permit.
recent_news: Dec 2023: Founder Clarence Pollard died. 2024-25: Pollard demoed Transfinder routing system with RISD board. Operates as DBA of Estes Transportation; FMCSA registration since 1999.
fit_notes: Attractive small tuck-in candidate: independent family-owned ~40-bus contractor with exclusive in-city RISD contract and charter permit. Founder recently deceased and apparent owner transition to Estes name suggests live succession dynamic. NM is a Summit expansion state; small but clean acquisition with district relationship.

name: Illinois Central School Bus
owner_or_parent: North America Central School Bus (NACSB) — portfolio company of H.I.G. Capital (H.I.G. Infrastructure acquired NACSB from Goldner Hawn in Aug 2021)
fleet_size_est: ~6,000-6,200 buses across NACSB platform (Aurora IL site is one of 46 facilities)
districts_or_customers: NACSB serves 120+ school districts across IL, IN, IA, KS, MO, TX, WI, PA, ID, and Ontario; Aurora hub serves western Chicago suburbs.
recent_news: Aug 2021: H.I.G. Capital Infrastructure acquired NACSB from Goldner Hawn. NACSB is the 5th-largest school-bus company in North America. Ongoing growth through tuck-ins.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target — already PE-owned by H.I.G. Capital as part of NACSB, the 5th-largest US school-bus platform and a direct Summit competitor. Exclude from outreach list.
